political.s: No direct corporate PAC or lobbying activity found under Cannondale's name. Parent company Pon Holdings is a Dutch conglomerate not heavily engaged in US political activity. Some cycling advocacy work documented supporting bike infrastructure.

charity.s: Cannondale and parent Dorel Sports have pledged support to cycling advocacy and community organizations including Bikes Belong, IMBA, and local trail infrastructure. Donations documented for collegiate cycling diversity programs and community partnerships.
charity.focus: Cycling advocacy, infrastructure, community access to cycling, diversity in collegiate cycling

environment.s: Manufacturing has shifted primarily to Taiwan, Vietnam, and China. Company claims commitment to sustainable materials and energy efficiency in manufacturing, but specific certifications (ISO 14001, etc.) not publicly disclosed. Environmental impact of overseas manufacturing mixed.

labor.s: Manufacturing conducted overseas (Taiwan, Vietnam, China) with limited public disclosure of labor practices. Failed to meet Connecticut job requirements on $3M state loan, resulting in $281K penalty. Glassdoor rating 3.6/5 stars with 69% would recommend. No OSHA or NLRB records found.

dei.s: Company explicitly commits to DEI with stated mission to 'celebrate diverse voices and create inclusive environment.' Women in senior leadership grew from 8% to 23% in recent years. Partnership with Good Co. Bike Club to promote cycling accessibility among underrepresented communities.

privacy.s: Limited public disclosure of data privacy practices. No specific privacy certifications or detailed policies found in search results. Standard e-commerce privacy practices assumed but not verified.

execPay.s: Not disclosed. Company is privately held subsidiary of Pon Holdings (Dutch family-owned conglomerate). No SEC proxy statements or public executive compensation data available.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
